decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game through countering spells, removing threats, and stalling with enchantments like Standstill. It also aims to control the board by utilizing Dust Bowl and Mishra's Factory. The combination of white removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and board wipes like Wrath of God helps maintain board presence. The deck also includes card draw and selection with Impulse and Fact or Fiction. Overall, the deck aims to control the game until it can win by outvaluing the opponent or resolving powerful threats like Decree of Justice.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing a combination of countermagic, board wipes, and land denial tactics. By playing cards like Mana Leak, Counterspell, Wrath of God, and Parallax Tide, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and slow down their game plan. Cards like Standstill and Mishra's Factory provide additional ways to control the tempo of the game. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing to deal with opposing threats. Overall, this deck focuses on maintaining control of the game and outmaneuvering the opponent.

The focus of this deck is to counter and stall opponent's plays using counter spells, while generating card advantage with cards like Fact or Fiction and Ophidian. It also aims to control the board with cards like Powder Keg and Parallax Tide, while providing some protection with Propaganda. The win condition comes from resilient creature lands like Faerie Conclave and Mishra's Factory, while also utilizing bounce spells like Chain of Vapor to disrupt opponent's board presence. Overall, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents through careful control and resource management.

In this deck, the goal is to quickly assemble the key combo pieces of Parallax Tide, Opalescence, and Replenish to create a powerful win condition. Attunement and Frantic Search help dig for these important combo pieces, while Parallax Wave can provide utility and protection. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Mana Leak, Swords to Plowshares, and Seal of Cleansing to control the game until the combo can be assembled. The deck's mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the spells.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through countering opponent's spells, card draw, and removal, while building up the power of Psychatog to eventually win the game. The deck leverages cards like Counterspell, Duress, and Smother to disrupt opponent's game plan and protect its own win condition. Gush and Accumulated Knowledge are used for card advantage and to efficiently cycle through the deck. The deck is designed to outvalue and outlast the opponent through careful resource management and strategic play.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy using a combination of countermagic, hand disruption, and removal spells. The deck aims to draw cards, generate card advantage, and protect its win condition, Psychatog, while using efficient spells like Gush and Accumulated Knowledge to maintain tempo. By controlling the game early on and efficiently using its resources, the deck can outvalue the opponent and secure a win with the powerful threat of Psychatog.

The deck focuses on playing aggressively with high-damage, low-mana cost creatures and burn spells to quickly deplete the opponent's life total. It includes a combination of direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate, alongside efficient creatures like Ball Lightning and Jackal Pup. Sulfuric Vortex and Cursed Scroll provide additional pressure, while Grim Lavamancer and Mogg Fanatic offer versatile removal options. The deck aims to maintain board control and apply consistent pressure to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.

This deck focuses on using Stifle to disrupt opponents while assembling the Dreadnought-Stiflenought combo. By controlling the game with counterspells and removal spells, the deck aims to clear the path for the Phyrexian Dreadnought to come into play with no drawback, creating a powerful and difficult-to-stop threat. The deck also incorporates card draw and board wipes to maintain card advantage and deal with opposing threats. With a mix of control elements and combo potential, this deck aims to secure victory through explosive and strategic gameplay.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with key enchantments like Parallax Wave and Parallax Tide using cards like Attunement and Frantic Search. By casting Replenish, all these enchantments can return to the battlefield, triggering powerful effects like recurring removal with Parallax Wave or turning all enchantments into creatures with Opalescence. The deck also includes counterspells like Mana Leak to protect the combo and seal of Cleansing to deal with opposing threats. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a sudden burst of powerful enchantments.

This Enchantress Combo deck focuses on utilizing enchantments to ramp up mana with cards like Wild Growth and Carpet of Flowers, while drawing cards and controlling the board with Enchantress's Presence and Parallax Wave. The deck aims to build a critical mass of enchantments to enable powerful interactions such as Opalescence turning all enchantments into creatures. The win condition is often achieved by overwhelming the opponent with a massive board presence or locking them out of the game with cards like Solitary Confinement. Overall, the deck prioritizes synergy between enchantments to create a strong and versatile strategy.

The deck focuses on fast-paced, aggressive gameplay by utilizing efficient burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate, along with aggressive creatures like Jackal Pup and Ball Lightning to deal direct damage to the opponent quickly. It also includes resource denial with cards like Fireblast and Price of Progress, as well as late-game reach with cards like Sulfuric Vortex. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with constant pressure and direct damage to finish the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of the deck is to use Oath of Druids as a control mechanism to cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore early in the game. The deck also focuses on disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Stone Rain and Wasteland while increasing its own board presence with cards like Mishra's Factory and Treetop Village. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Sphere of Resistance and Sylvan Library to control the game tempo and maintain card advantage. Pyroclasm and Naturalize are included to deal with opposing threats and disrupt their strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The deck focuses on playing efficient creatures like Wild Mongrel, Mogg Fanatic, and Jackal Pup, while also utilizing burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate to clear the way for attacking. The deck also includes utility cards like Naturalize and Grim Lavamancer to deal with opposing threats. With a mix of creatures and burn spells, this deck aims to close out the game quickly and efficiently before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize cards like Ill-Gotten Gains, Intuition, and Dark Ritual to set up powerful combos and win conditions. The deck includes various card draw and mana acceleration spells to help smooth out draws and generate the resources needed to execute its game plan. With a mix of cantrips, rituals, and land support, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm opponents with powerful plays that exploit synergies between its key cards.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, Vindicate, and Wrath of God to deal with threats on the board. Additionally, hand disruption cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy help to keep the opponent's resources in check. The deck also has access to card advantage through Skeletal Scrying and Decree of Justice, while also having access to powerful finishers like Eternal Dragon and Akroma's Vengeance to close out the game.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of aggressive goblin creatures. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey to cheat creatures into play and Goblin Warchief to reduce costs, the deck aims to flood the board with threats early on. Cards like Siege-Gang Commander and Goblin Sharpshooter provide additional removal and utility, while the overall strategy is to attack relentlessly and finish the opponent off before they have a chance to stabilize. The deck also includes disruption like Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's resources and control the tempo of the game.
